self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can improve Engine planning quality by making integration planning consume typed extraction, adaptation, improvement, and proof output instead of flattening that stage onto the same base planning input.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result turns the already-landed extraction-to-adaptation-to-improvement-to-proof seam into a fourth bounded Architecture implementation target for integration-stage chaining.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP case, the Engine integration proposal still used only planningInput even though extraction, adaptation, improvement, and proof already produced typed output inside processSource().
self_improvement.expected_effect: The Engine can now carry forward earlier stage boundaries into integration planning without reopening Runtime or host work.

decision.rationale: The current Engine shape justifies one bounded integration-stage chaining slice as the next product-owned Architecture implementation target.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
